selection_summary.strategy: Built from the existing 20-job current_generation_selection and deterministically topped off by 12 additional wave1 jobs, with step_by_step weighted to 8 cases and all other task types at 6.

judge_role.statement: Judge output is treated as audit/secondary signal only; this wave is for practical user_sim + answer shadow validation, not rollout gating or promotion.

provider_usage.note: Approximate sums from provider-reported usage in saved OpenRouter responses; not billing-grade values.

qualitative_findings.finding: The answer starts on-source but breaks off before the final target state, so the procedural completion is missing.
Two distinct procedures are merged into one sequence, which makes the guidance ambiguous for a step_by_step request.

This job flipped from approve in the earlier 20-job GPT-5.4 run to reject here because the new answer duplicated the same 3-step procedure verbatim, not because the judge changed without answer drift.
One of the newly added top-off step_by_step jobs stayed fully procedural, complete and well-calibrated.

largest_score_shift.interpretation: Answer regression, not same-answer judge drift.

topoff_12_job_slice.interpretation: The newly added cases are largely stable; the only reject is again a step_by_step completeness failure.

recommendation.user_simulation_and_answer: Robust enough for larger shadow waves than the earlier 8/20 subsets, but still not clean enough to consider rollout-like gating because step_by_step remains materially fragile.
recommendation.judge: Sufficient as a secondary/audit judge at this wave size; still not suitable as primary gate.
recommendation.next_step: Prefer another larger shadow wave plus targeted prompt work for step_by_step answer generation; judge retuning is lower priority than fixing procedural answer completeness and duplication.
